A cloud of ink blooms around a broken statue on the sea floor. Long arms reach from the dark behind it, using the silt as cover.
A broad shark circles below the party, vanishing whenever lanternlight turns its way. It strikes only after the current pulls everyone apart.
A hulking shape drags a chain through a sunken hull and bellows through the water. It has claimed the wreck and treats every diver as a thief.
The water ahead squeezes into a cold, fast stream between stone teeth. Reading the current before entering keeps the party from being scattered.
A vent opens with a roar of boiling mineral water. The plume is brief, but hot enough to scald anyone too close to the stones.
A deep call rolls through the water from nowhere nearby. The sound carries through bone and armor, turning the dark sea briefly into a cathedral.
A bronze bell tolls once from inside a drowned shrine, though nothing visible has touched it. A coastal priest wants the bell found before it rings again.
A trembling pocket of air clings beneath an overturned boat on the sea floor. Someone is alive inside, but the hull shifts with every current.
Green lights rise from a trench and arrange themselves like signal lamps. The pattern repeats a name, then drifts downward into crushing dark.
Wide fans of sponge pulse with pale light around a mineral vent. Only the outer lobes can be cut without killing the colony.
A brittle black coral grows where cold current meets warm vent water. Snapping the wrong branch sends razor fragments through the silt.
Thick kelp lines sway from rocks like anchor chains. The young lengths are edible and useful, but the old ones shelter snapping things.
Hundreds of tiny lights gather in a ball around the party's shadows. A fine net can take a portion before the cloud blinks out.
Pale crabs march along a ridge in a steady line, each one carrying a pebble of ore. Taking too many breaks the line and scatters the rest.
Transparent eels knot themselves around a warm current. They are almost invisible until they twist against a darker stone.
Dark nodules cover the seabed like scattered fruit. They are easy to lift, but too much silt hides the richest patch.
Green-blue copper stains the stones around a smoking vent. The useful crust flakes away only while the vent sleeps between pulses.
A hollow of giant shells holds pearls under a layer of drifting sand. The shells close slowly but powerfully when disturbed.
A ship's beam remains wedged beneath a coral arch, protected from worms by cold water. Freeing it risks bringing the arch down.
A dead coral trunk has grown straight and hard as worked wood. It can be cut into staves if the living reef around it is spared.
Old kelp has twisted into thick cables around a wrecked anchor. Properly dried, the strands serve as tough lashings.
Cold shapes follow the party along a trench wall, waiting for the lantern glow to narrow. The attack comes from below with barbed nets first.
A garden of whale bones rises from the dark silt, each rib hung with trophies. The merrow guardian treats every passerby as a thief.
Black smoke belches from a vent and hardens into spiteful little shapes. Their boiling spray scalds before the fight properly begins.
Pearl chips mark an old sea elf route across a field of identical stones. Reading the pattern reveals which stones are sleeping predators.
The seafloor drops away into a blue-black shelf where pressure tightens around armor and lungs. A controlled descent keeps panic from taking over.
A distant song trembles through a buried hull and makes every loose nail answer. The melody is sad, precise, and clearly not made by wind.
A pearl beacon used by sea elves has gone dull inside a kelp-choked cage. Restoring it means discovering who wanted the route hidden.
A sealed courier tube lies beside a skeletal hand gripping coral. The message inside names a second courier who should have arrived days ago.
A merchant wreck rests upright in impossible calm beneath a surface storm. Its hold contains survivors' marks and one fresh lantern.
A voice taps in code from inside a kelp maze whose fronds tighten around metal. The prisoner knows the way to a sahuagin shrine.
A barnacled idol on the trench lip opens one stone eye whenever blood enters the water. Someone has been feeding it on a schedule.
Long black kelp grows around warm vents, glossy and tough. The tender inner fronds must be cut before heat wilts them.
Anemones glow blue along a basalt wall, their tips useful in alchemical dyes. Touching the wrong tentacle numbs the hand.
Dense sponges filter mineral-rich water near a wreck. Squeezing them too hard ruins the useful fibers.
A crab with a shell like black plate wedges under a vent ledge. It is slow, strong, and very unwilling to leave.
A dead giant clam has left thick nacre plates fused to stone. Clean plates are valuable if lifted before they crack.
A vent has blown black glass across the seafloor. The sharp pieces hold heat and must be gathered with care.
An ancient anchor chain lies half-buried in silt, each link dark with cold iron. Freeing useful pieces stirs the whole slope.
A wreck beam has turned dark and dense under years of pressure. It can be cut, but every saw stroke draws curious fish.
Rigid kelpwood stalks grow tall enough to brace a small hull. Cutting mature lengths keeps the root crown alive.
A fallen mast lies cradled in coral that has grown around the grain. Freeing it whole takes patience and a gentle blade.
A violet lure swings above a dark trench mouth, drawing small fish into reach. Catching the angler means avoiding the sudden bite behind the light.
Pearlscale fish graze along a sunken statue's shoulders. They scatter into the statue's mouth whenever bubbles rise.
